channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Detroit Mayor Mike Duggan, a longtime Democrat, will run for Michigan governor in 2026 as independent
item.description: Detroit Mayor Mike Duggan, a longtime Democrat, says he'll run for Michigan governor in 2026 as an independent because “there are a lot of people in this country who are tired of both” the Democratic and Republican parties.
item.dc:creator: Corey Williams, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Migrants nervous over Trump plans for mass deportations
item.description: "I am traumatized. If I don't get the appointment (with border officials) I will go back," one woman said.
item.dc:creator: Xavier Walton, NewsNation

item.title: Freshman Focus: Republican Rob Bresnahan, who ousted six-term House Democrat, reveals how he did it
item.description: Rep.-elect Rob Bresnahan of Pennsylvania discusses his priorities for the next Congress with Fox News Digital after he unseated six-term incumbent Rep. Matt Cartwright.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Minnesota budget update shows $1.1B less than prior forecast
item.description: The economic report released Wednesday morning by state budget officials projects a downturn, with only a $616 million cushion expected by the end of the next budget period. The new data will guide lawmakers when budget deliberations begin in January.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Inside RFK Jr.'s nonprofit's legal battles over vaccines and public health
item.description: The nonprofit Children's Health Defense that Kennedy led has filed nearly 30 federal and state lawsuits since 2020, many challenging vaccines and public health mandates.
item.dc:creator: Shannon Bond, MPR News

item.title: Democratic challenger Adam Gray flips California's 13th Congressional District in nation's final House race
item.description: Democrat Adam Gray has won California's 13th Congressional District race, defeating incumbent Republican Rep. John Duarte.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Federal judge accuses President Biden of attempting to 'rewrite history' in Hunter Biden pardon
item.description: U.S. District Judge Mark Scarsi issued a scathing rebuke of President Biden's claim that his son, Hunter Biden, was unfairly treated during his criminal tax case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers urge judge to toss his hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump’s lawyers formally asked a judge Monday to throw out his hush money criminal conviction, arguing that continuing the case would present unconstitutional “disruptions to the institution of the Presidency.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Dems help pave way for Donald J. Trump Avenue in Miami-Dade County after historic election victory
item.description: Miami-Dade County honored President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday by unveiling a sign for the street that was renamed in his honor, with the backing of Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Leticia Perez on way to reelection to 5th District Supervisor seat after final vote count released
item.description: Kern County 5th District Supervisor Leticia Perez appears headed to reelection, leading challenger Kim Salas by 133 votes in final vote count released by elections officials Tuesday afternoon.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump vows to block Japanese steelmaker from buying US Steel, pledges tax incentives and tariffs
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump is underscoring his intention to block the purchase of U.S. Steel by Japanese steelmaker Nippon Steel Corp., and he's pledging to use tax incentives and tariffs to strengthen the iconic American steelmaker.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump’s DEA nominee withdraws from consideration just days after being picked
item.description: President-elect Trump’s choice to lead the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) announced on Tuesday he is withdrawing from consideration just three days after Trump made his nomination known.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Florida Sheriff Chad Chronister withdraws as Trump's nominee to lead DEA
item.description: The Florida sheriff that President-elect Donald Trump nominated to be the administrator of the U.S. Drug Enforcement Administration has withdrawn his name from consideration.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Facebook still silent after suddenly banning then reinstating this popular gun manufacturer
item.description: The popular American gun manufacturer Smith & Wesson says that it is still being kept in the dark after its Facebook account was suddenly suspended last month.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Senate Democrats stick with Schumer as leader
item.description: Chuck Schumer faced no opposition in the party leadership elections, in which Illinois Sen. Dick Durbin was also re-elected to the No. 2 spot and Minnesota Sen. Amy Klobuchar became the new No. 3.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: St. Cloud appoints first Somali American to city council
item.description: On a unanimous vote, the city council appointed Hudda Ibrahim to fill a vacancy on the seven-member board. She replaces Jake Anderson, who was elected St. Cloud mayor last month.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: Trump appointments set off special elections to fill House seats: What to know
item.description: The move by President-elect Trump to tap several House Republicans to fill positions in his Cabinet and beyond has set off a fresh round of special elections next year to replace them. Trump chose Reps. Mike Waltz (R-Fla.) and Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) to be his national security adviser and U.S.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Vakil, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps investment banker Warren Stephens for UK ambassador
item.description: President-elect Trump announced on Monday that investment banker Warren Stephens would serve as U.S. ambassador to the United Kingdom.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump taps investment banker Warren Stephens for UK ambassador
item.description: “Warren has always dreamed of serving the United States full time. I am thrilled that he will now have that opportunity as the top Diplomat,” Trump said.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: At Supreme Court, vaping may be frowned upon, but that could change with Trump
item.description: The focal point of the case is 2009 law enacted by Congress that gives the Food and Drug Administration a mandate to curb the availability of nicotine products for minors.
item.dc:creator: Nina Totenberg, MPR News

item.title: Biden pardons his son Hunter despite previous pledges not to
item.description: President Joe Biden has pardoned his son, Hunter, sparing the younger Biden a possible prison sentence for federal felony gun and tax convictions and reversing his past promises not to use the extraordinary powers of the presidency for the benefit of his family members.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas Mayor Carolyn Goodman prepares to leave office, ending an era
item.description: Carolyn Goodman, who has served as Mayor of Las Vegas for 13 years, was honored with the renaming of Civic Plaza in her honor, and looks forward to the future with her husband Oscar.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News
